properties.description:
A hands-on session starting at 3pm ET for asset managers and institutional allocators building AI agents into their IR, RFP, and diligence workflows.

We'll go behind the scenes of a modern diligence AI agent - how it's designed, where it fails, and what's actually implementable today - from tagging content libraries and responding to DDQs for asset managers to orchestrating ODD, ESG, compliance, tax, and legal review for allocators.

Expect practical frameworks, real use cases, and a live demo you can take back to your team - ideal for teams looking for low-risk, high-impact ways to bring AI into due diligence.

This event is a part of NYC TechWeek - a week of events hosted by innovators to bring together the tech ecosystem.
properties.title: AI Agents for Allocators and Asset Managers

properties.description:
Connect Smarter at Tech Week

Have you ever attended a large event with hundreds—or even thousands—of people, and felt unsure about who you should talk to?

We’re solving that problem. Instead of leaving it all to chance, your personal AI agent will first connect with other participants’ agents, helping you discover the most relevant people for you to meet. By the time you walk into the room, you’ll already know who your best matches are.

Here’s how it works:

Now – May 31, 9:00 PM → Sign-up is open.

Jun 1, 8:00 AM → You’ll receive an email with your personalized matches for Tech Week, along with a summary of the conversations your agent had on your behalf.

Jun 1, 2:00 – 4:00 PM → Join us for an in-person networking session at a cafe in New York, where you can finally meet your matches face-to-face.

We look forward to seeing you there!

properties.description:
Age and Identity verification is full of deepfakes. It is getting harder and harder to distinguish honest actors from fraudsters. 

The solution isn't that we use better AI tools to detect deepfakes - that is playing a losing battle. Rather we should be using authoritative sources and cryptography to verify user's identity. 

That is what Reclaim Protocol does (https://reclaimprotocol.org) - an open source verification system that uses zero knowledge proofs to verify users' identity. 

Come learn more, and discuss with others solving the same problem. Each participant will be hand picked, to maintain high density of like minded people. 

Looking forward to seeing you!

In this masterclass, we’ll introduce Mellea, an
open‑source framework that treats large language models,
not as chat partners, but as programmable components
inside real applications. You’ll see how AI behavior can be
broken into clear, composable steps using the Instruct–
Validate–Repair pattern, with structured outputs and
grounded retrieval built in. The payoff: predictable,
auditable, and reproducible AI. Combined with smaller
open‑weight models like IBM’s Granite models, developers
are seeing performant, trustworthy results at a fraction of
the cost. We’ll close the session with a real‑world case
study that turns a stack of construction PDFs into a fully
cited cost estimate and report.

You didn't move halfway across the world to get tripped up by Delaware vs New York and C-Corp vs. LLC decisions. Join Acclime and Herrick for a no-BS lunch session where legal, tax, and market-entry experts break down exactly what international founders get wrong in their first 12-24 months stateside—and how to fix it before it costs you a round.

FULL DESCRIPTION

The U.S. is the biggest market in the world, and the easiest place to make a six-figure mistake before you've closed your first customer or investment round.

Entity structuring that triggers double taxation. IP ownership gaps that spook investors. Hiring decisions that create liabilities you didn't know existed. Most international founders don't discover these landmines until they're already expensive to unwind.

This Lunch & Learn at Herrick's Murray Hill offices brings together the legal, tax, and cross-border specialists who clean up these messes daily—except this time, they're helping you avoid them entirely. properties.description:
​Leaders are regularly asked to make high-stakes decisions: where to invest, which interventions to prioritize, and how to allocate limited resources for the greatest impact. And these decisions only grow harder as systems become more deeply intertwined, consequences less predictable, and the stakes higher. Yet the tools we rely on weren't built for this. Large language models and conventional ML share a critical blind spot: they can't predict how a system will respond to an intervention it has never encountered before. To navigate today's complexity, we need tools that can reason beyond historical precedent and anticipate the downstream effects of the choices we make.

Anthos is a simulation platform that lets investors, philanthropies, and policymakers stress-test decisions before they commit capital or sign policy. It ingests the relevant literature, then applies neuro-symbolic causal inference to model the second- and third-order consequences spreadsheets and standard forecasting tend to miss. Non-technical users can run "what-if" scenarios through conversational AI and quickly see whether an investment thesis or policy proposal holds up under pressure.

Join us for an intim…
properties.title: Causal Foresight with Anthos: Navigate Uncertainty with Better Decision-Making.

properties.description:
A year ago, the question was "which model?"

Today it's "which model for which job — and how do they wire together?"

Devs building real AI pipelines have stopped picking favorites. They're routing across dozens of models through Cloudflare Replicate, then composing the output with editing and control layers like Bria — background removal, generative fill, structured JSON prompts (FIBO), video tools with 1:1 audio preservation.

The new craft isn't prompting. It's orchestration.

For one night during NY Tech Week, we're putting the engineers building this layer in the same room. Come for working patterns, not slideware.
Every RSVP gets free Cloudflare Replicate + Bria API credits — leave with enough to wire something real that night.

What you'll take home
— How teams are routing tasks across model providers in production
— Where editing/control APIs fit in the stack (and where they don't)
— Cost, latency, and quality tradeoffs nobody puts in the docs
— What's actually shipping vs. what's still a demo

Schedule
5:00 — Doors, drinks, mingling
5:45 — Fireside chat + audience Q&A
6:15 — Food, drinks, more conversation
7:30 — Wrap

Speakers
Chris MacDonald — Head of Solutions, Bria…

What if you could feel how giving shapes the nervous system in real time?

Totems of Hope is a neuroaesthetic immersive installation exploring presence, attention, and human connection through sound, spatial design, and non-invasive neurotechnology.

As participants move through the experience, physiological patterns subtly influence an evolving sensory environment — where sound, interaction, and neuroadaptive systems create a living feedback loop between inner state and space.

This is not a talk.  
It is something you step into.

As part of the experience, participants collectively prepare food resources that are redistributed to a local community, linking inner experience with tangible impact. The event gathers artists, technologists, founders, researchers, and cultural innovators around emerging conversations at the intersection of humanity and technology.

The experience will take place at Duca Studio by Duca Sartoria, bringing an additional layer of craftsmanship, elegance, and contemporary Italian design into the experiential environment — creating a dialogue between embodied technology, aesthetics, and human expression.

Do you have deep industry insight and wonder if it could become a venture-backed business?

Are you ready for your next chapter to be something you build, not just something you join?

The Leap: From Executive to Founder is an invite-only intimate dinner hosted by Build Momentum and Merrill Lynch for senior executives, operators, and domain experts exploring the move from leadership into company building.

You have built teams, carried responsibility, solved hard problems, and seen an industry from the inside. At some point, the question shifts from How do I keep climbing? to What do I actually want to build?

This gathering is for people standing at that edge.

Life is short. The best ideas do not wait forever. And the move from executive to founder is rarely simple. It asks for conviction, courage, and a willingness to trade certainty for possibility.

We’ll gather a small group of experienced leaders, founders, investors, and builders for a candid dinner conversation about what it takes to turn expertise into a company, move from idea to execution, and make the leap from executive to founder.

Who this is for:

● Sen…

Getting press coverage in 2026 is both more important and more difficult than ever. One story in the right outlet can do more than a year of paid ads - but that email you sent to a journalist about your startup is one of hundreds of pitches just like it.

Join us for a breakfast panel with journalists and comms leaders from major media and tech companies including Yahoo and Vox Media who will share what actually lands coverage in the age of AI slop.

Founders, apply for the a limited spot in the workshop to follow and put what you've learned into practice.

properties.description:
A 30-Minute Panel + Networking at Tavern Community Coworking.
As a founder, you're constantly deciding what to do yourself, what to hire for, and what to buy or build a tool for. But AI is changing that equation—fast.
Entire categories of work that used to require a hire, an agency, or a vendor can now be handled by AI tools. At the same time, the need for human judgment is getting higher, not lower. There’s a critical difference between outsourcing a task and outsourcing judgment. 
The risk isn’t just delegating work—it’s losing control of decisions that define your company. Get it wrong and you're either burning cash or burning out. 
This panel is about getting it right.
We'll dig into how to figure out which parts of your business need you, which are better handed off to an external specialist, and which problems already have a tech solution waiting for you. This discussion will help you move faster without losing control of the things that actually matter.
We're bringing together founders who've been in the weeds, operators who've built the systems, vendors who sell the solutions, and investors who've watched it play out to give you insights from experience you can use for y…

properties.description:
For years, quantum computing was seen as a breakthrough perpetually just out of reach. That perception is beginning to shift. Scientists are now combining quantum and classical systems to address problems that become prohibitively complex at scale, especially in molecular modeling, chemistry, materials science, and energy. This session will explore what has enabled recent progress, what quantum computing can deliver today, and how the gap between long‑term promise and practical impact is starting to narrow. 

Moderator: Karmela Padavic-Callahan, Physics Reporter at the New Scientist​

Confirmed panelist:​
- Juan de Pablo, Anne and Joel Ehrenkranz Executive Vice President for Global Science and Technology, New York University & Executive Dean, NYU Tandon School of Engineering​
- Osama Raisuddin, Quantum Algorithms Research Scientist, Rensselaer Polytechnic Institute​
- Dr. Andrea Goldsmith, President, Stony Brook University
- Jerry Chow, CTO of Quantum-Centric Supercomputing and IBM Fellow

The doctor’s office is no longer the default entry point into healthcare.

GLP-1s are prescribed over text. Blood panels are ordered by consumers before symptoms appear. Gyms are evolving into longevity clinics. AI is becoming a first-line triage tool. properties.description:
[2026 Tech Week] Early-stage founders rarely think about IPO.
It feels distant — something for “later.”

But in reality, exit is not a final milestone. It’s a filter applied from day one.
Every early investor is implicitly asking:

*Can this company scale into something public markets will understand?
Will institutional capital show up at exit?
*Is the path structurally clean — or broken before it begins?
*That lens shapes which companies get funded, how they’re valued, and what paths remain open.

And here’s the part most founders don’t realize: NASDAQ’s Capital Market Equity Standard requires only a two-year operating history.Meaning many companies could be IPO-ready within ~36 months — if the right capital and structural decisions are made early.

This Panel Connects the Full Capital Lifecycle. Bringing together perspectives across every stage:

*A pre-IPO investor who writes the checks that signal IPO readiness
*A public-market investor who decides whether to buy on opening day
*A recently IPO’d operator who just navigated the transition

Together, they map the critical inflection points across the journey —
what to build, what to prove, and what fundamentally changes when t…

properties.description:
New model drops. New framework. New "this changes everything" thread. By the time you've integrated one tool, three more have made it obsolete.

So we asked ourselves a different question: what doesn't change? Across enterprise teams, across codebases, across the hype cycles. Which engineering practices actually hold up when the underlying models rotate every quarter?

Artur Skowroński and Tomasz Lelek spent a year shipping AI-assisted code across enterprise clients, watching what broke and what survived, then committed the patterns to print in Vibe Engineering: Best practices, mistakes, and tradeoffs, co-published with Manning Publications.

Join Artur and Tomasz for a discussion panel and community mixer, and learn about:
- Where AI earns its place in the delivery cycle, and where it creates new debt 
- Vibe coding beyond the individual contributor: what changes at team and org scale
- Keeping quality and trust intact when half your code has a co-author 
- Staying fast without losing control of what ships

And if you are interested in writing a book yourself, Artur and Tomasz will also talk about:
- Picking the right topic, so that the trend is still relevant when the book is…
properties.title: How to Write a Book on AI in Enterprise SDLC While Patterns Keep Changing

properties.description:
Most AI products die before they're built. 💀  

Pitch decks pile up. Side projects get half-shipped. The thing that was supposed to change everything quietly becomes a folder of unused Notion docs and a vibe-coded prototype with zero users.

This is the workshop that fixes that.

* * *

In 2 hours, you'll move through the same methodology I've used at work, taught at universities (e.g. Brown University, Hunter College), and in my 6-week cohort to 80+ professionals.

80% of them launched an MVP or advanced their career within 90 days.

You'll work through:

→ Problem Pressure Test: find a problem worth solving (and walk away from three that aren't)

→ Decision Value Stack: pressure-test whether anyone will actually pay for what you're building

→ Build / Skip Matrix: what to ship in v1, what to defer, what to kill.

You'll leave with a 1-page pressure-tested concept brief for your own AI product, and a clear sense of what "build it" actually looks like, whether "it" is a startup, a side project, or an internal tool at your company.

Learn by doing.

* * *

👋 About Alisha Outridge

Former CTPO at TuneCore, led AI projects that drove +25% revenue and +30% engagement across 500K+ cr…
properties.title: AI Founder Playbook: Stop Building AI Products No One Pays For

properties.description:
Your company has data. But it doesn't have a memory. 

On June 2nd, we fix that in one live session. 

You're going to build the first agents of your AI Operating System - your company's second brain. A single layer of intelligence that connects to your CRM, call recordings, Slack, playbooks, and answers the questions you keep paying people to answer manually. 

No coding. No abstract "learn about AI" slides. 

You'll walk in with your tools, workflows, and integrations. 

You'll walk out with with a single working dashboard pulling from all your data sources that you can interact with.

What you're taking home:
- Working agents connected to your actual data, built by you

properties.description:
AI agents are moving from demos to production. They're executing payments, writing code, accessing sensitive data, and making decisions autonomously. But the safety infrastructure hasn't kept up. Guardrails are probabilistic. Failures are silent. Prompt injections can hijack agent behavior. And enterprises are stuck between "move fast" and "don't blow up."

The missing piece is cryptographic guarantees. Not better prompts. Not more filters. Mathematical proof that an agent action complies with your policy before it executes. See docs.icme.io

This workshop teaches you how to build agents that enforce policies with formal verification, resist prompt injection and hijacking, and prove compliance before every action. Hands-on, technical, and applicable to any agent framework.

Data Vandals Newsstand
As part of the MTA’s Vacant Unit Activation Program, the Data Vandals created a data-art kiosk and conversation space on the downtown 6-train platform at 51st & Lexington. 

When the space is open, it will be a place for conversation, with weekly surveys, changing exhibitions, in-person events, talks, music, and a free newspaper. Through weekly sticker votes, charts, maps, and quirky drawings, riders can share their experiences with others, opening lines of communication and dialogue.

properties.description:
For decades, the systems that underpin work — benefits, healthcare, and financial stability — were built around a single assumption: one job, one employer, one steady income.

That assumption is breaking.

Today, millions of Americans earn across multiple roles, platforms, and income streams — a structural shift often referred to as the "microshifting workforce." But the systems designed to support them haven't kept up.

As this shift accelerates, the gaps are becoming harder to ignore: lost coverage, fragmented care, and systems that weren't designed for movement.

At the same time, new technologies, particularly AI, are beginning to reshape how individuals navigate fragmented systems, manage care, and coordinate across multiple sources of income.

Join us for a conversation with leaders Ellyn Shook, former CHRO of Accenture; Susan Podlogar, former CHRO of MetLife; and Tim Massa, EVP and CHRO of Kroger. Moderated by Bradley Tusk, founder and CEO of Tusk Ventures, with Felix W. Ortiz III, founder and CEO of Smirk Health.

This panel brings together leaders across tech, healthcare, and the future of work to explore what happens when foundational systems fall out of sync—and what'…

properties.description:
Building a company is hard.
Turning it into real wealth is even harder.

During NYC Tech Week, we’re bringing together a small group of founders, operators, and builders for a candid conversation on what actually happens between scaling and exit—and what most people get wrong along the way.

This is not a surface-level panel.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

We’ll cover:
• What changes between Series A → B → Exit
• Mistakes founders make with equity, taxes, and liquidity
• How to think about personal wealth while still building
• What operators wish they did earlier

Featuring:
– A current Series B founder in the trenches
– A founder who has successfully exited
– Advanced planning specialist from UBS

Expect a high-signal room, honest conversation, and real connections.

Limited capacity. Approval required.
properties.title: NYC Tech Week: Builders & Exits

properties.description:
HardTech Forum hosted by MistyWest (www.mistywest.com) and presented by Intent (https://www.withintent.com/)

---

Since Kevin Ashton coined "Internet of Things" in 1999, the promise of real-time tracking has finally become practical, built on RFID, BLE, cellular telematics, and satellite connectivity. Devices now run 10 years on a single battery, and new systems are experimenting with Bluetooth signals detected directly by satellites.

After our April 9th Vancouver HardTech Forum on The Internet of Moving Things, MistyWest and Intent are bringing the conversation to NYC with The Internet of Building Things. Construction is one of the hardest environments to digitize—chaotic, temporary, and GPS-denied—but autonomous drones, LiDAR, and adapted fleet sensor stacks are changing that fast. This HardTech Forum asks: now that we can see the physical world with unprecedented resolution, where does the real value get created, in the hardware, the data, or the platforms that turn sensor feeds into decisions?

---
SPEAKERS

Ben Williams - COO at Exyn Technologies (www.exyn.com/)

Ben is the COO/CPO of Exyn Technologies, pioneering GPS-denied autonomy in multi-modal robots. Previously, he…
properties.title: The Internet of Building Things

properties.description:
Step into The Retro: a cocktail experience surrounded by artifacts from companies that didn't make it. Each piece tells a story about what was built, what mattered, and what comes next.  

This isn't a cautionary tale or a roast. It's a room that brings order to an ending most of the industry avoids talking about. Because what was built still matters—even when it ends. 

Enjoy custom cocktails and appetizers, explore curated displays throughout Pearl Box's first floor, and join the conversation about the full lifecycle of building, including closure.

Raise a glass to what didn't make it—and what comes next.

About SimpleClosure: 
SimpleClosure is the shutdown partner for founders and startups navigating the less-talked-about side of entrepreneurship. When it’s time to properly close a company, SimpleClosure handles the paperwork, legal filings, compliance requirements, and final steps founders are often left to figure out on their own. What is traditionally a confusing, months-long process becomes a clearer path forward, so founders can close cleanly, and get back to building. To learn more, visit SimpleClosure.com.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted b…

properties.description:
When adopting customer-facing AI, companies often start with service. But now, the most forward-thinking teams are taking a more holistic approach. They're not just using an AI Agent for service – they're collapsing the walls between support, success, and sales, to build a more a connected customer experience.

For Clay, the transformation is already underway. They've embedded GTM engineering into Ops teams to drive support efficiency at scale. Support Engineering is placed at the intersection of CX teams to reduce volume by fixing issues and building internal tools. Support and Success are becoming more collaborative— moving from focusing volume management to building durable customer relationships.

Fin is building toward the same future — a single Customer Agent that delivers a seamless customer experience — from sales to service to success.

Join Declan Ivory, VP of Customer Support at Fin / Intercom, and George Dilthey, Head of Support at Clay, for an honest conversation about what scaling CX with AI actually looks like in practice — the org changes, the use cases you haven't tried yet, and where this is all heading.

This one's for teams who are past the starting line and…

properties.description:
You closed your first customers on grit. The problem is grit doesn't compound.

Most founders hit the same wall: product-market fit is real, revenue is coming in, but growth feels like pushing a boulder uphill every single morning. Your GTM motion is a patchwork of one-off plays, manual outreach, and tribal knowledge locked in someone's head. You know it won't survive the next stage. You just haven't had time to fix it.

This workshop transforms that in real time. You will walk out with 1) clarity on the systems mindset you need to scale, 2) a working piece, and 3) a downloaded DragonflySkill for you to use during the workshop with Claude.

Where should you start? RSVP to receive via email a free GTM assessment that takes 15 minutes. This diagnostic assessment will help you determine your GTM stage.

Come ready to work!

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

properties.description:
MVP to Launch: Content Credentials for Creators, Builders, and Brands 

Join Adobe for a special gathering that goes beyond the AI conversation to explore what meaningful transparency looks like across the creative ecosystem: who made something, how it was made, and why that matters, whether you're an illustrator, a builder, or a startup founder scaling to enterprise. 

Content Credentials is the global standard for digital provenance, securely attaching verifiable information to content, including photos, videos, audio, and generative AI, proving the origins of media and how it was created, composited, and edited. This information travels with the content and can be surfaced across apps, platforms, and websites. 

Drinks and heavy appetizers are on us! Given limited capacity, we'll follow up to confirm your registration.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

Why come:
- Hear from practitioners who’ve built AI audience intelligence inside real companies — and hit the limits
- Get an honest take on where AI and digital twins create real value and where they produce confident nonsense
- Walk away with a clearer picture of how the best teams are making faster, better decisions about their customers

properties.description:
Women’s healthcare is a trillion-dollar market still running on 1990s trial-and-error. Nowhere is this more broken—or more lucrative—than IVF, where patients routinely spend $60k+ out-of-pocket on guesswork.

Why are women blindly taking 79 different supplements when their actual biomarkers only require 18?

Join VitalHelix Happy Hour during NY Tech Week for the exclusive unveiling of our predictive AI platform. We are replacing the guesswork with data-driven accuracy to deliver fewer cycles, optimized protocols, and faster pregnancies.

🧬 Who is in the room:
• Investors & VCs: See the data, meet the founders, and look under the hood of a high-growth FemTech platform disrupting a massive private-pay market.

• Our Pilot Cohort: We are hosting a select group of women (currently in IVF, post-failed cycles, or successfully pregnant) who are validating our platform in real-time.
Come for the cocktails, stay for the paradigm shift. Let’s talk scalability, data moats, and changing the future of fertility.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com

Every panelist comes with something specific: a pricing model they got wrong, a technical decision that set them back months, a sales motion they scrapped and rebuilt. No slides. No safe takes. Just the decisions that actually shaped what they are building today.

If you are an early-stage founder or GTM operator working on an AI product right now, this room will be full of people solving the same problems you are.

One question on every RSVP: "What are you building with AI right now?" That is how we keep it worth your time.

Join Norton Rose Fulbright during New York Tech Week for an intimate happy hour at the intersection of innovation, intellectual property and artificial intelligence.

As AI reshapes how companies create, compete and commercialize, a strong IP strategy is no longer just about protection. It's about turning your intellectual assets into meaningful revenue while navigating an increasingly complex legal landscape. This evening will explore how founders, technologists and legal leaders can move beyond defensive IP strategies to actively monetize innovation in the AI age, without stepping on the rights of others.

Partner Johanna Schmitt and Counsel Sam Eichner from Norton Rose Fulbright's Brands Group, along with Stephanie DeBrow from the firm's Patent Disputes practice, will kick off the evening with a brief presentation covering timely topics, including:

Monetizing your IP
•	Copyright in the age of AI: Who owns AI-generated content? How are courts and regulators approaching authorship, fair use and training data liability?

Take part in panel unpacks the bias “under the hood" of the Gen AI and how we can address it. Hosted in partnership with Vine Ventures.

About the Speakers:
Daniel Kelley is the Senior Director of the Anti-Defamation League (ADL) Center for Technology and Society (CTS). 

Raquel Saxe leads strategy and program management at Jigsaw, a Google incubator that builds technology to increase human agency in the AI era.

Margarita Lyadova is the Host & Creator, People Jew Wanna Know'

properties.description:
AI is not just a technological shift - it’s a restructuring of the labor market.

White-collar roles are compressing. Entry-level pathways are breaking. Entire categories of work are being reorganized faster than new ones are being created.

At the same time, demand is rising for real, task-based capability - often in areas the market has historically overlooked or misunderstood.
We’re entering a moment where:
> Output is becoming abundant
> But signal on human capability is increasingly scarce

The structure of work is changing.
Capital hasn’t fully caught up.

This salon brings together a curated group of founders, investors, and operators to explore how labor markets are evolving - and where capital is likely to flow next.

We’ll discuss:
> Where displaced talent goes - and where new demand is actually emerging
> What replaces traditional entry pathways - and who controls access to opportunity
> Which sectors expand vs. contract - and why the market may be mispricing both
> Where talent is being undervalued - and how that creates asymmetric opportunity
> What new infrastructure is needed - and which companies could define it

This is not a surface-level “future of work” panel…
properties.title: Light Dao Salon: Who Wins in the Age of AI? Labor Markets and Capital in Transition (NYC)

properties.description:
In partnership with the Healthcare Club of NY and Caseflow AI (usecaseflow.ai) for NY TECH WEEK.

New York is about to decide something the rest of the country will be watching: whether chatbot companies should be on the hook when their AI gives medical, legal, or mental health advice that causes harm.

Senate Bill S7263, sponsored by State Senator Kristen Gonzalez, would make chatbot operators civilly liable when their AI provides "substantive" advice in domains covered by professional licensing law. That's 14+ licensed professions plus the practice of law. Disclaimers don't save you. A "you're talking to a bot" notice doesn't save you. And the private right of action means anyone harmed can sue.

Supporters say it's common sense: if a human pretending to be a doctor can be prosecuted, a chatbot that hallucinates a medical license shouldn't get a free pass. Critics say the bill is vague about what counts as "substantive" advice, puts nonprofit and government chatbots in the same legal crosshairs as OpenAI, and ends up taxing the people who most need low-cost information (tenants, patients, small business owners) on behalf of licensed incumbents who don't love the competition.
B…

properties.description:
Your AI prototype works on your laptop. Now what?

Most non-technical founders hit a wall when their demo needs to become a real product. Auth, costs, latency, observability, evals, deploys, rollback. The things engineers take for granted are the things that scare you most.

In this 90-minute working session, we'll take a small group of non-technical founders through the same checklist Tequity uses to ship our clients' products from prototype to production:

- Audit what's actually production-blocking vs. what feels scary but isn't
- Map your real costs at 100, 1,000, and 10,000 users
- Identify the 5 things that will break first when you launch
- Write a one-page production readiness brief you can hand to a dev team (or use to hire one)

This is hands-on. Bring your prototype (or a screenshot of it). You'll leave with a checklist you can act on this week.

Who this is for:
- Non-technical founders who have a working prototype and don't know what "production" actually requires
- Founders who built something with Claude, Cursor, Lovable, or Bolt and are stuck on what comes next
- Anyone who's been told "you can't ship that" but doesn't know which parts are real

Limited to 10 peo…
properties.title: From Prototype to Production for Non-Technical Founders

properties.description:
GIDE Hackathon @ a16z NYC Tech Week

An elite, in-person hackathon hosted at the downtown Manhattan offices of Perkins Coie LLP (1155 6th Ave, 22nd Floor) during a16z NYC Tech Week.

Who Can Compete:
1. Up to 50 participants. (Limited Participants)
2. Developers, students and founders.
3. Prior GIDE hackathon winners auto-qualify (must confirm by Sunday, one week before).


Format:
30-min orientation → 5 hrs of in-building competition → 30-min judging → live presentations → awards for top 3

Platform Requirements:
Core solution must be built in GIDE and reviewable in a locally downloaded copy. MCPs, APIs, and external services are fair game, but teams must demo live to judges.

Judging:
30% Technical merit
30% Inventive use of GIDE
30% Effectiveness in solving the problem
10% Creativity

Schedule:
8:00 – 9:00 AM : Breakfast & Check-in
9:00 – 9:30 AM : Kickoff, rules, challenge reveal
10:00 AM : 3:00 PM : Hack
12:00 PM : Lunch 
3:00 PM : Judging begins
4:00 PM : Awards ceremony

Prizes announced live at the event.

Hosted in Perkins Coie LLP office

properties.description:
Most diabetic foot amputations are preventable.
So why are 150,000 Americans still losing a limb every year?

The science has been settled for over a decade. The technology exists. The patients are waiting.
What's missing is the deployment layer and that's exactly what's about to break open.

Battery-free wearables. Mass-market manufacturing. New regulation pushing the industry past disposable, battery-dependent devices. 
For the first time, the economics of diabetic foot prevention work at the scale of the problem. 

This is one of the biggest unsolved categories in medtech:  tens of billions in annual US healthcare spend, millions of underserved patients, and a 24-month window before the category winners are picked.

Join us for a NY Tech Week 2026 breakfast roundtable on what it actually takes to make prevention work at scale.

Around the table: clinicians on the front lines, payers tired of paying for what shouldn't happen, investors scanning for the next medtech category, founders building in wearables and chronic care, plus the regulators and patient advocates shaping what gets deployed. 

[REGISTER NOW →]

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs…

properties.title:
From Detection to Deployment:
A New Era for Diabetic Foot Care

properties.description:
Capital to Customers: Why smart funding, clean ownership, and retentive CX matter.

How do founders turn capital into customer demand and drive scalable growth?

This session brings together leading voices across venture capital, fintech, and customer experience to break down what actually drives efficient growth. Speakers from FJ Labs, TrueBridge, Airbus Ventures, Hustle Fund, Zendesk, Efficient Capital Labs, and Bright Ventures will discuss powerful strategies for capital sourcing and allocation as well as customer acquisition, retention, and experience. 

We’ll focus on how smart funding (venture debt, revenue-based financing, strategic venture capital, secondary liquidity), clean ownership (cap table optimization, data-driven underwriting), and high-performing customer experience (AI-powered support, success, and operations) work together to extend runway, reduce drag, and build momentum in practice.

properties.description:
Fast Growth, Real Pressure: Leading AI-Driven Organizations to Alignment and Performance

AI is accelerating everything: product cycles, expectations, growth and competition. The pressure to grow fast and deliver results is constant.

But here’s the reality:
Most companies don’t break because of bad strategy. They break because their teams can’t stay aligned as they scale up.

As organizations grow, execution slows, priorities clash, and leaders become bottlenecks. What looks like a technical problem is often a leadership one.

In this interactive workshop, Ashveena Gajeelee (Founder, The Adaptive Leader; Harvard-trained leadership educator) will help you see what’s really happening inside your organization and what to do about it.

Through real-world tech scenarios, you’ll learn how to:
- Identify the unseen obstacles that emerge with fast growth
- Navigate resistance, misalignment, and competing priorities
- Lead under pressure: when authority, data, or AI alone aren’t enough

Built for founders, product leaders, and VCs navigating high-velocity environments where performance must hold, even without clear answers.

If your organization is growing but execution is getting harde…

properties.description:
Last week, we tried breaking into 30 startups.

20 of them had severe vulnerabilities.

AI is writing code faster than ever - and nobody is securing it. Founders are shipping new features, instead of building a digital fortress. 

The result? 

A generation of products that look polished on the surface and are wide open underneath.

This isn't a theory talk. We're doing live hacks.

Bring your laptop. Bring your ego. Leave with a clearer picture of where your product actually stands.

What to expect:
- How hackers are approaching AI-generated codebases differently
- The most common (and embarrassing) vulnerabilities we keep finding
- Live breakins - on volunteer products in the room, and public startups
- What a real security baseline looks like for a startup that's already shipped

Who this is for:
Founders - technical or not - who have a live product and genuinely don't know if it's secure. Especially if you've leaned heavily on AI to build it.

What this is not:
A vendor pitch. 
A beginner's intro to cybersecurity. 
A safe space for your tech team.

---

Seats are limited. We need volunteers willing to let us attempt to break into their product live. Sign up in the RSVP notes…
properties.title: The (Ethical) Guide To (AI) Hacking

properties.description:
💡 What’s the Ugly Truth
People are not going to shop the way they do now forever. The shift is already starting. Consumers will increasingly ask AI agents to search, compare, recommend, and in some cases buy for them.
That means brands cannot rely on the old playbook alone. If discovery moves from browsers and marketplaces to AI-led interfaces, brands risk losing attention, pricing power, customer data, and margin.
The real question is not whether agentic commerce is coming. It is whether your brand will know how to compete when the buyer is no longer a person clicking through your storefront, but an AI making the shortlist.

🎯 Why You Should Attend
-Learn what agentic commerce actually looks like right now, not just the hype.
-Understand how shopping behavior may shift over the next 5 to 10 years.
-Get clear on what parts of the old ecommerce playbook still matter, and what is starting to break.
-Learn where brands should invest now to stay discoverable, defensible, and profitable.
-Avoid expensive mistakes around channels, data, pricing, and customer acquisition.
-Meet the founders, operators, and researchers shaping this shift in real time.

🙋 Who's Getting In This Room
-Bran…
properties.title: Ugly Talk: Can Your Brand Thrive in Agentic Commerce?

properties.description:
Gender-based violence (GBV) remains one of the most underreported human rights violations globally, disproportionately affecting women, youth, and vulnerable communities who often face barriers to reporting, limited access to support systems, and a lack of trust in institutions.

This session brings together Red Dot Foundation Global, Catcalls of NYC, Humanitarian OpenStreetMap Team and Integrated Fundraising, in collaboration with The NYC PIT Pop Up, an activation of CUNY Public Interest Technology Lab, to explore how technology can make invisible harms visible, amplify survivor voices, and strengthen public accountability.

To make the experience more participatory and hands-on, the session will be delivered as an immersive solution showcase and rotating demo lab rather than a traditional panel.

Participants will explore practical, community-driven technologies and creative approaches that address gender-based violence, strengthen civic participation, improve public decision-making, and support safer, more inclusive communities.

This event is part of #NYTechWeek - a week of events hosted by VCs and startups bringing together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com

​Across the American Midwest and broader Heartland, a powerful innovation corridor is emerging. Cities like Columbus, Cleveland, Cincinnati, Detroit, Pittsburgh, Chicago, Indianapolis, and St. Louis are producing world-class research, ambitious founders, and globally competitive startups—often operating with exceptional capital efficiency and deep technical talent. As infrastructure investment, manufacturing resurgence, and frontier technologies converge, the region is becoming one of the most compelling new frontiers for venture capital in the United States.

​The question facing investors is no longer if venture will expand beyond the coasts.

properties.description:
Loading Future State: She's Been Waiting Long Enough

You know her.

The version of you that walks into the room differently. That knows her worth before anyone names it. That builds from clarity instead of fear.

She's not ahead of you. She's been running alongside you the whole time, waiting for you to stop performing the version of yourself you've outgrown.

Loading Future State is the only event at NY Tech Week built around that gap between who you are and who you're already capable of being.

Experience on-stage conversations and interviews with the women who already closed the gap, from billion-dollar unicorn founders, multi-exited investors, operators in the thick of it who stopped waiting and started becoming. A dedicated founder and investor lounge where the real conversations happen off script. Partner activations designed to make your future self impossible to ignore. And a room full of New York's most ambitious women who came to do more than network.

properties.description:
A casual gathering for engineers, students, and researchers who are interested in AI and computational research. 

Both the research-curious and veteran researchers are welcome. Celebrate the start of summer with an ice cream social!

Come meet the marimo founding team, learn about our newest open-source releases (hint: we give Claude access to live state in a notebook!), and connect with others in the research community.

Featured Speakers: Myles Scolnick (marimo Co-Founder, CTO) and Trevor Manz (marimo Founding Engineer)

About marimo: marimo is an open-source reactive Python notebook designed specifically for reproducible computation, rapid data manipulation, and interactive science communication. 

Unlike Jupyter, marimo notebooks are reactive, stored as pure .py (Git-friendly), executable as scripts, and easily deployable as web apps. 

marimo has been downloaded over 12 million times and is used for research and AI/ML development at companies like BlackRock, Shopify, OpenAI, Cloudflare and more.

properties.description:
As robotics and automation accelerate across industries, most conversations remain abstract and focused on technology capabilities rather than real world deployment. 

Robotics adoption in U.S. manufacturing is accelerating. Nearly 400,000 industrial robots are now operating in U.S. factories, with tens of thousands added each year. At the same time, manufacturing continues to face a labor gap measured in the hundreds of thousands of open roles, with millions more projected this decade. But adoption is not the same as success. Most automation challenges do not fail at the level of technology. They fail on the factory floor, where people, process, training, safety, and operating constraints
determine whether systems work or stall.

Humans + Robots & The Factory Floor brings together operators, robotics companies, and systems leaders to explore what happens when automation meets the real world of production.

This session moves to examine the lived experience of integrating robotics into active manufacturing environments.

Why This Matters Now
Robotics adoption is increasing, but deployment challenges remain widespread. Many automation efforts fail not because of the technology it…
properties.host: New York Robotics, American Manufacturing Futures Institute
properties.title: Humans, Robots & The Factory Floor –

properties.description:
Agent Harnesses in Practice

AI agents are advancing quickly, but turning them into dependable business systems requires more than model access. It requires a harness: the specifications, controls, validation layers, tooling, and operating patterns that make agent behavior useful, repeatable, and safe.
This New York Tech Week panel brings together practitioners working at the intersection of AI, software delivery, and enterprise operations to discuss how organizations are approaching this new engineering discipline. We’ll break the space into clear categories, examine where current tools fit, and explore practical patterns for moving from prototype to production.
The conversation will focus on real-world implementation questions: how teams define agent scope, structure workflows, validate outputs, introduce human oversight, and apply these systems inside complex existing environments. 
We are inviting engineering leaders, founders, product and platform teams, and anyone trying to understand where agent systems create real value today.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week…
properties.title: Agent Harnesses: What It Takes to Make AI Agents Work in the Enterprise
properties.venue_address: Union Square W, New York, NY 10003

properties.description:
More and more, the primary user of a product isn't a person — it's an LLM reading docs, calling tools, and doing work on someone's behalf. The human has moved one layer up.

Zo and Tanagram are building into this shift from opposite ends. Zo gives individuals a personal AI cloud computer where agents act on their behalf. Tanagram shapes what coding agents produce inside engineering teams, turning tribal knowledge into guardrails the agent follows.

Join the founders for a candid conversation on designing for a user that reads everything, hallucinates confidently, and never sleeps — what reliably works, what reliably breaks, and how product thinking changes when tool schemas are the new UI.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

properties.description:
Wednesday, Jun 3

5:00pm – 9:30pm

Hosted at Studio 260
260 W 36th St, New York, NY

For decades, the path to managing institutional capital has been gated not solely by skill, insight, or discipline — but by access to infrastructure, networks, and institutional machinery.

The world’s leading investment firms did not simply identify talented investors. They built operating systems around them: research workflows, risk frameworks, execution infrastructure, portfolio controls, and organizational discipline that transformed differentiated theses into real investment operations.

Podium exists to make that operating layer accessible to a new generation.

We believe AI, software, and modern infrastructure are fundamentally reshaping who can build, validate, and scale investment strategies. The next wave of exceptional portfolio managers may emerge not only from traditional hedge funds or investment banks, but from high-agency founders, systematic thinkers, domain experts, and technical builders who possess real edge — but have historically lacked the institutional environment to prove it.

This program is designed to identify and cultivate those individuals.

properties.description:
As AI enters its next phase—from copilots to autonomous systems—the convergence of Robotics (Embodied AI) and Large Language Models (LLMs) is reshaping how work gets done in the physical and digital world.

In this new paradigm, capital is not just funding software—it is underwriting end-to-end intelligent systems that integrate perception, reasoning, and action. From industrial automation and service robotics to agentic workflows and real-world deployment, a new category is emerging:
👉 “Service-as-Software” powered by Robotics + LLMs

This private roundtable convenes leading LPs, GPs, family offices, and frontier founders to explore how global capital can align with this shift—and where the most asymmetric opportunities lie.

1️⃣ Capital Flows into Robotics & Embodied AI
Where is smart money moving? From hardware-enabled AI to full-stack robotics platforms, we examine capital allocation strategies across the stack.

2️⃣ LLMs as the Control Plane for the Physical World
How LLMs are evolving from copilots to decision-making engines—powering robotics, automation, and real-world workflows.

3️⃣ LP–GP Alignment in Frontier AI Investing
New expectations on fund construction, timeline…

Kabir Daya is Chief Digital Officer at Thriveworks, one of the country's largest behavioral health platforms, bringing the perspective on an operator deploying AI at scale.

Kathleen Leger is a triple board-certified physician in Internal Medicine, Nephrology, and Critical Care Medicine at TeamHealth, bringing a frontline clinical perspective on where AI actually meets the patient. 

Claire Siegel leads product at Humanaut Health, a longevity and preventive medicine platform building AI-enabled care, bringing the perspective of a builder who makes daily decisions about what AI touches in a patient relationship.

A candid conversation for high-earning women in tech who are doing all the “right” things with money - but still don’t feel strategic.

You’re earning well. You’re saving. You’ve opened the accounts.
But if you’re honest… it doesn’t feel like a system. And it’s not clear what it’s all building toward.

In this session, we’ll walk through a practical framework to:
	•	take your finances from scattered to structured
	•	align your money with your actual life goals
	•	and start using your income to create real flexibility - not just long-term savings

So when you think about your next chapter - whether that’s a break, a pivot, or something new - you’re actually in a position to choose it.

Includes a 45-minute session followed by Q&A and networking over wine and light bites.

properties.description:
Artificial Intelligence is moving beyond the digital world and into the physical one. This shift, often referred to as Physical AI, is quickly becoming one of the most important frontiers in the field.

Leading AI labs, global technology companies, and a new generation of startups are building the foundations for this transition. But while progress is rapid, one question remains central: what does it actually take to make Physical AI work in practice?

NVIDIA is building the compute platforms and simulation tools. ALLSIDES is building the 3D data layer and infrastructure.

Join Franz Tschimben (CEO, ALLSIDES) and Adam Brasic (Senior Solution Architect, NVIDIA) for a fireside chat and reality check on where Physical AI stands today, what is already working, and where real value is emerging.

The conversation will explore how simulation, infrastructure, and AI come together in real-world applications, and what it takes to turn Physical AI into a viable business. It will touch on platforms such as Omniverse and Isaac Sim, the role of high-quality 3D data, and why data is increasingly becoming the bottleneck in this space.

This is a curated gathering of founders, investors, develop…
properties.title:
Physical AI: 
A Reality Check on AI’s Next Frontier by ALLSIDES & NVIDIA

properties.description:
Today, cash management is a board-level conversation and the stakes are high. With rates where they are, every basis point on idle cash matters, and so does every assumption about counterparty risk, liquidity, and duration. The finance leaders getting this right are turning treasury from a cost center into a quiet source of competitive advantage.

Join a16z and Moment — the AI operating system for fixed income and treasury management — for an intimate evening with a small group of CFOs, controllers, and finance leaders during NY Tech Week. We’ll share a dinner together and go deep on the practical questions every modern finance team is wrestling with:
- What are finance teams actually getting from their legacy bank relationships and where are fintechs filling the gaps? 
- Is yield optimization still the priority, or are efficiency and visibility becoming the bigger unlock?
- How are AI and modern infrastructure changing how finance teams manage and optimize their cash balances?

Expect candid discussion, peer learning, and the kind of conversations that don’t happen at larger conferences. Limited seats — finance leaders and controllers only.

properties.description:

You’ll taste a curated lineup of wines completely blind, submit your feedback in real time, and watch as your palate becomes data — revealing collective preferences, patterns, and top performers.

Then, we reveal everything.

After the reveal, you get to revisit the wines with full context, experiencing firsthand how perception shifts once labels, stories, and pricing are introduced.

This isn’t just a wine tasting — it’s a live demonstration of how consumer data can directly determine retail decisions. By turning individual opinions into actionable insights, we’re building a more transparent, democratized approach to wine buying — where the crowd, not gatekeepers, decides what earns shelf space.

properties.description:
Most high-performing women have learned to work around it. The two weeks of brain fog, mood dysregulation, fatigue, and physical symptoms that arrive every month like clockwork, and disappear just as quietly. They push through. They reschedule. They apologize for things that aren't their fault.

What they haven't been told is that this has a name, a mechanism, and a cost.

PMS and PMDD affect an estimated 3 to 8 percent of women of reproductive age at the clinical level, and up to 13 to 18 percent experience symptoms severe enough to impair function without meeting the full diagnostic threshold. The economic impact is estimated at $4,333 per woman per year in lost workplace productivity. And the last meaningful clinical innovation for this condition was the birth control pill in 1960.

This panel brings together founders, clinicians, and women with lived experience to have the conversation that workplaces, investors, and the healthcare system have been slow to have. We'll explore the emerging science, including the gut-hormone-brain axis now being studied as a key driver of premenstrual symptoms, what it means for women's performance and careers, and why this is one of the most…
properties.title: The convo the workplace is too polite to have

This event is a fireside chat + mixer for a curated room of founders, researchers, physicians, and angel investors in the neurotech space to gather to exchange ideas, forge meaningful relationships, and discuss the state of the evolving neurotech field. By bridging the gap between biological intelligence, AI, and governance systems, we aim to catalyze the breakthroughs that will define the next decade of cognitive enhancement and computational neuroscience.  

This event will be part of a series of NeuroNYC's newest NeuroAI initiative. Large scale AI models are being built for the brain, neural interfaces are moving from lab to clinic, and AI architectures are increasingly drawing inspiration from neuroscience. As these technologies advance, so do the ethical and governance questions they raise: who owns neural data, and what guardrails should guide AI systems modeled on or decoding the brain? These challenges demand expertise that no single discipline can provide alone. New York City sits at a unique crossroads of neuroscience, AI, medicine, engineering, ethics, and policy talent, and that's what NeuroAI aims to bridge.

properties.description:
The creator economy made a lot of people rich. Just not always the creators.

Join us during NY Tech Week 2026 for an evening that takes that problem seriously. The Overlooked Economy is a live demonstration and conversation built around a simple premise: the most valuable advertising inventory in digital media has been sitting inside creator content for years, unpriced and unclaimed, and the creators who built it have never had a mechanism to access what it was worth.

FullScale brings its AI-powered product placement technology into the room and runs it live, on real content, in front of the audience. You will watch it surface placement opportunities and attach dollar values to inventory that existing ad infrastructure has never been able to see. Then a curated panel of creators, brand marketers, and investors will have the honest conversation about why that gap existed, who it cost the most, and what changes now that the infrastructure has caught up.

Expect a focused guest mix, a room shaped with intention, and a conversation with something real to say. No pitch competition. No keynote theater. Just a live demonstration and the kind of discussion that tends to keep going aft…

properties.description:
Loop is an invite-only supper on the recursive patterns behind the physical economy — control loops, feedback loops, scaling laws.

It’s for founders and investors working in manufacturing, field services, construction, energy, logistics, defense, and humanoids — the companies where revenue is earned at an address, not a URL.

Most tech dinners happen in the world of bits — SaaS metrics, model benchmarks, ARR curves. Loop is the other table. It’s for the people building and backing the companies where revenue is earned at a job site, a factory floor, a warehouse, or an address on a map.

The conversation has one spine: the recursive patterns that govern physical revenue. Control loops. Feedback loops. Scaling laws. The physical world doesn’t compound like software — but it does compound. Understanding how is the whole game.

Twelve seats, one long table, a pop-up chef, and natural wine from growers around the globe. Five courses built on a single scaling rule. An Escher print on the menu, a Duchamp quote chosen for each guest, and a quiet personalization on each plate. No decks, no panels, no pitches.

Loop is hosted by Allometry — the agentic operating substrate for physical re…

properties.description:
As AI rapidly reshapes the commerce landscape, “agentic commerce” has emerged as a hot topic, yet an often misunderstood concept. This panel, hosted by Juan Pellerano-Rendón, CMO of Swap, will cut through the noise to define what agentic commerce actually means in practice, and just as importantly, what it does not.

Bringing together perspectives from brand, technology, and commerce infrastructure, this conversation will focus on how AI agents can empower merchants and strengthen the direct connection between brands and their customers, rather than replacing it with big tech models designed only to operate within their own infrastructure. 

Moderated by the host and creator of Fashion & Founders, Victoria Smith, the panel will include Justin Felizzari, Founder of Cueva, and Gina Lombardo, VP of E-Commerce at Retrofête, alongside Juan, to dive deeper into how leading brands are transforming traditional e-commerce sites into conversational shopping experiences and leaning into the world of agentic commerce. 

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.
properties.title: Agentic Commerce: What It Is, What It Isn’t – and Who It Should Serve

- Jesse Carey – Product Strategy & Ops, Stripe

Speaker bios:
Dori Yona
Dori started SimpleClosure after facing a tough moment in his own business when he needed help closing down a company but couldn't find good advice anywhere. After running an app called Earny that millions of people used, he knew there had to be a better way to help business owners close their companies. His new company, SimpleClosure, now helps hundreds of other business owners shut down their companies faster and cheaper than traditional methods. Based in Santa Monica with his family, Dori works to show that closing a business doesn't have to be scary or hard, and that learning from tough experiences can lead to helping others.

Longevity science is at an inflection point. Foundation models are learning the language of biology, aging clocks are quantifying human health with new precision, and a new generation of therapeutics, diagnostics, and clinical platforms is emerging to act on those signals.

AI x Longevity Summit, the inaugural flagship summit from the NYC Chapter of Longevity Global, explores how artificial intelligence is compressing the distance between discovery and real-world intervention. This year’s theme, Signal to Intervention, focuses on the systems, infrastructure, and translational strategies shaping the future of longevity science and healthcare.

Hosted at BioLabs @ NYU Langone in the heart of New York City’s biotech corridor, the Summit convenes researchers, clinicians, founders, investors, and engineers building the next generation of longevity infrastructure.

properties.description:
Your AI & Product works. But does your user trust it?

Most founders optimize for capability. The best founders optimize for human experience. The difference is whether someone uses your product once or makes it part of their life.

In this 90-minute working session, we'll take a small group of product founders through the same process Tequity uses with its clients:
- Map the user's world — who they are, what they're doing when they encounter your product, and what they're feeling
- Identify the trust gaps — where your product asks users to make a leap of faith, and whether you've earned it
- Build a design brief — a one-page document that captures the decisions your product needs to make about how it presents itself to humans

This is hands-on. Bring your product (or your idea). You'll work on your own company with guidance from our team.

You'll leave with a design brief you can hand to your designer (or Claude), your dev team, or yourself — and start using immediately.

Who this is for: 
— Founders at the zero-to-one stage building AI products 
— Technical founders who know their product works but aren't sure it communicates well 
— Anyone who's gotten the feedback "the produ…

Bring Your Product. We'll Break It.

Live adversarial testing for 10 AI-native founders. 60 minutes. Real vulnerabilities. Coffee provided.

Your product ships fast. Your attackers move faster.

Most early-stage companies think security is something to solve later, after scale, after growth, after fundraising. But modern products are being probed from day one by bots, automated scripts, AI-assisted attackers, and increasingly sophisticated threat actors.

In this 60-minute live session, we’ll simulate how real attackers evaluate a product and show you where your system breaks before the internet does.

Using controlled offensive security methods, the Tequity Security Ops team will:

— Map your exposed attack surface across product, infrastructure, workflows, and user behavior
— Demonstrate how seemingly small vulnerabilities compound into major security risks
— Identify trust and security gaps across authentication, permissions, APIs, AI systems, internal tooling, and operational workflows
— Show how attackers think, move, escalate access, and exploit assumptions inside modern products
— Help you understand what security maturity actually looks like for a zero-to-one company

The Rise of the $26T Brain Economy: What Founders, Corporates, and Investors Need to Understand to Build the Next Unicorn

AI is scaling faster than human biology can adapt to it.

This session explores how:

* cognitive overload,

properties.description:
AI APOCALYPSE OR SALVATION? THE CLOCK IS TICKING.

You might be feeling a knot in your stomach lately; the same feeling you get thinking about climate change, watching democratic norms fall like so many dominoes, or seeing kids glued to their screens, frying their attention spans, having algorithms permanently impact their brain development—a feeling of helplessness. 

We post. We protest. We panic. But nothing changes. 

What if there were a solution? 

This goes beyond a presentation - it's an invitation to reclaim power.

Spartacus.app is a weapon that ordinary people like us have been waiting for.  Drawing on insights from Game Theory, it combines conditional pledges and temporary anonymity to create something revolutionary: a means for the powerless to gain power. 

Think about it: Why don't people speak up? Because it's not "worth it." The risk is too high, the reward too uncertain. 

But what if you knew you weren't alone? What if you knew exactly how many others would stand with you? 

That's what Spartacus does. It builds critical mass. And numbers equal leverage, protection, and impact. 

Whether you're building communities, organizing unions, gathering signatures, exp…

HealthBridge is an AI-powered database tackling one of the most overlooked problems in American healthcare, where millions of low-income and non-English-speaking families cannot reach the care that exists around them, blocked by confusing insurance policies and a complete lack of direction. Co-hosted with Youth Civic Service, a nonprofit dedicated to empowering young people through community engagement, this event brings together roughly 50 attendees in Chinatown, NYC for a live demonstration of the HealthBridge platform, a formal presentation by the founding team, and an open conversation about what it actually takes to build health technology for the communities most often left behind. Refreshments provided. HealthBridge was invited to present at the U.S. Congress on April 21st & 22nd, 2026. Pending Co-hosts with the Association of Chinese American Physicians & Congressman Tom Suozzi.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups to bring together the tech ecosystem. Learn more at www.tech-week.com.

The room will bring together portfolio managers, allocators, venture investors, founders, operators, and technical builders across public markets, private markets, AI infrastructure, and semiconductors.

properties.description:
*Light bites and beverages will be served thanks to our friends at R1!*

Most health AI events follow the same formula: researchers talk about what they built. Founders talk about what they're selling. The audience leaves wondering how the two connect.

We want to break that. We're bringing together scientist-founders to show how the worlds of AI research and healthcare product converge. How do you take frontier science—whether that's foundation models, behavioral insights, or clinical validation—and turn it into a product that clinicians and patients actually use? What do you keep? What do you cut? Where does rigor meet velocity?

For founders scaling health AI, for investors evaluating science vs. hype, for researchers building for impact: this is the hour where the real work gets named.

We're kicking off with a fire-side chat with Martin Seneviratne (R1) for a fireside chat on moving healthcare AI from theory to operation. What does it take to deploy AI in environments where accuracy, compliance, and scale all matter at once? We’ll unpack how R1 is turning messy clinical and financial data into working systems—and what that means for builders who want to take AI beyond demos…

We're bringing together ~150 founding engineers, ML builders, and product engineers to build agents that solve something real - not a demo, not a slideware prototype, something you'd actually use on Monday.

Come with a laptop, a teammate (or find one here), and a problem you've been meaning to solve. Leave with a working agent, a small crowd that saw you ship it, and - if your build clears the bar - a prize.

Prize to be announced soon.

properties.description:
Without Project REDS, the current predevelopment timeline is:
1) Site Selection & Feasibility: 6 months to 2 years (includes market analysis and locating land).
2) Due Diligence & Land Acquisition: 6–9 months (includes soil testing and environmental checks).
3) Investment Scoring: 1 to 6+ months (varies by NOI, property value, and cap rate).

With Project REDS, the current predevelopment timeline is:
1) Site Selection & Feasibility: 10 minutes (includes market analysis and locating land).
2) Due Diligence & Land Acquisition: 10 minutes (includes soil testing and environmental checks).
3) Investment Scoring: 10 minutes (varies by NOI, property value, and cap rate).

This workshop will allow the audience to gain hands-on experience on how to use generative AI to acquire properties more swiftly than before! This workshop will enable developers to acquire land, multifamily, and commercial properties swiftly, test scenarios, and visualize community impact before breaking ground!

Learn more about us: https://www.projectreds.info/

description:
A live wearable tech demo + networking event at Tech Week 2026
Ever finish a workout, a yoga flow, or a stretch routine and wonder if you actually did it right? Most of us have no idea when our bodies are compensating, tilting, or drifting off course. LumbarGuide makes the invisible visible — and tonight, you get to feel it yourself.
This hands-on demo event puts a working wearable biofeedback system directly on attendees. No slides. No theory. Just real-time haptic feedback the moment your body moves wrong.
The evening features:
🏋️ Plank Station — "How Long Can You Actually Hold It Right?" Think you know how to plank? Strap on the device and find out. The wearable detects the moment your lower back starts to sag, tracks your hold time, and buzzes when the clock runs out. No more guessing. No more cheating yourself.
🧍 Scoliosis Simulation — "Now Imagine Doing This Alone, Every Day" Watch a short clinical rehab video — as many times as you want, just like a real patient would after a clinic visit. Then try to replicate the movement from memory. The sensor will tell you if your correction went the right direction, i…

properties.description:
The startup org chart is breaking.

Founders are under pressure to move faster, stay leaner, and build more with less. But the old answer (hire a full executive team, add more headcount, and hope the org chart catches up) no longer works for most early-stage companies.

At the same time, two new forms of leverage are changing how startups operate: AI agents and senior fractional.

AI agents open up a nearly infinite set of opportunities to automate tasks, redesign workflows, monitor execution, and support day-to-day operations.

Fractional leaders give founders access to experienced operators across go-to-market, product, operations, finance, engineering, design, people, and strategy without the cost or commitment of premature full-time hires. 

But for early-stage founders, the hard part is knowing how to put those pieces together.

Which roles need senior human judgment? Which functions should be fractional before they become full-time? Which tasks are ready for agents? What should stay founder-led, what should be delegated to expert operators, and what should become an AI-enabled workflow?

This NY Tech Week session helps founders answer those questions.

Felix, the AI CEO of…

properties.description:
CREWASIS presents: Human in the Loop in the age of Agentic AI
https://crewasis.ai/

Cindy Cook, Chair of the New York Angels will be providing an interesting perspective on how Agentic AI investments are being looked at from a preseed/seed standpoint.  

Kelsey Deans will provide a perspective on implementing Agentic AI solutions in enterprise companies.

Sharon Joseph, CEO of CREWASIS, will provide a perspective in building an AI native company and adding in agentic AI. CREWASIS is a Techstars company.

Agentic AI has made it trivial to build.
It has made it effortless to generate.
It has made it faster than ever to launch and optimize.

It has not made it easier to stand out.
It has not made it easier to earn trust.

Enterprise teams are now operating in a sea of Agentic tools, each promising automation, each claiming intelligence.

But most are producing more noise, not better decisions.

So the real question is not how to use AI Agents and test them for success.

It’s how to use Agentic AI to actually improve marketing outcomes inside enterprise environments.

This session brings together operators and investors at the front lines of that shift to unpack what’s working, what…
properties.title: CREWASIS Presents: Human in the Loop: Marketing in the Age of Agentic AI for Enterprise

properties.description:
Sponsored by Havas

Infinite Impact Lab is a hands-on workshop for professionals, operators, and team leaders who are doing meaningful work but struggling to clearly explain the value they create.

This workshop is for you at any stage of your career.

Whether you are getting back into the job market after a layoff, preparing for a promotion, trying to get stronger in your current role, pivoting into a new role or industry, returning after a career break, or getting ready for performance reviews and salary conversations, one thing is true:

Being capable is not enough if your impact is hard to see.

Many professionals are doing the work, solving problems, supporting teams, improving processes, driving outcomes, and creating value every day. But when it is time to talk about that work, they default to tasks:

“I manage projects.”
“I support customers.”
“I run reports.”
“I lead meetings.”
“I handle operations.”

The problem is, tasks do not always tell the full story.

Infinite Impact Lab helps attendees turn everyday execution into clear, measurable business value.

In this session, attendees will use GTM’s framework — Task → Skill → Impact → Proof → Transfer — alongside AI to un…

properties.description:
​Generative Media wants to be free!

The best generative media models will be open. Open weights mean faster iteration, deeper customization, and real transparency about what's inside. Closed APIs can't offer that — and the market is starting to figure it out.

​Bria, fal, and LTX are building on this bet. Bria ships creative control and rights-clear models designed for commercial production. LTX builds open-source foundational video and image models pushing the frontier on quality and efficiency. fal makes them all run fast at scale. Together, we think this is what the generative media stack looks like going forward — open, fast, and commercially safe.

​Join us during NYC Tech Week for an evening of talks, a panel, and honest conversation about where open generative media is headed.

​🗓 Schedule

​5:00 PM — Doors open + drinks

​6:00 PM — Talks + panel + drinks

​6:45 PM — Networking + drinks

​7:30 PM — Wrap + drinks

properties.description:
What happens when a nationally ranked debater 
goes up against the AI he built?

Kevin Chen — peak ranked #40 nationally out of 3,800 teams in 2026 — will debate live against Coach ARIA, the AI debate coach he built from the ground up. Kevin runs the case. ARIA hits back. 
The audience watches a real competitive round play 
out between a national-circuit debater and the 
machine trained to judge him.

This is not a panel. This is not a pitch deck. 
This is a live debate — one human, one AI, one 
resolution, on stage at NYC Tech Week. After the 
round, Kevin opens the hood: how ARIA was built 
on GPT-4o, how years of competitive experience 
were engineered into a structured evaluation 
system, and what it actually takes to build AI 
that domain experts actually trust.

Built by a debater. Now it's fighting back.

properties.description:
Your startup's name is doing more work than your pitch deck — and most founders get it wrong. . Drinks, good people, and a take-home naming kit. Eli Altman, the mind behind Okta, Waymo, and Codex, breaks down the framework.

---
Eli has named some of the most recognizable companies & product in tech — Okta, Codex, and Waymo. His view: the biggest mistake isn't picking something embarrassing, it's picking something safe. And the naming process itself is really just forced clarity on what your company is, who it's for, and how it needs to show up in the market.

At this event he's breaking down what actually makes a name do GTM work: why most founders try to make a name say too much, what real differentiation looks like, and how to build something the market can actually hold onto.

Every attendee leaves with the Go Name Yourself Kit — A Hundred Monkeys' 90-card deck so you can put Eli's process to work the moment you walk out.

Come for the talk. Stay for the drinks and the room — founders, accelerators, and early-stage VCs all figuring out brand, GTM, and how to build something that actually sticks.

This event is a part of #NYTechWeek—a week of events hosted by VCs and startups…
properties.title: Don't Call It That — Naming, Brand & GTM with Eli Altman

properties.description:
Join us for an evening of drinks, networking, and a conversation the accounting profession is overdue for.

General-purpose AI is impressive. It can summarize, draft, and reason across an enormous range of tasks. But accounting and tax aren't general problems. They're governed by rules that change, edge cases that carry liability, and judgment calls that no model gets right just by being large. That gap, between what AI can do and what accounting actually demands, is where the most consequential work in the profession is happening right now.

This panel brings together thought leaders, firm leaders from the profession, and the builders working at that frontier to share what's working in practice, where the hype still outpaces reality, and what separates AI that holds up in an accounting environment from AI that doesn't.

If you're leading a firm or advising one, this is a conversation worth being in the room for.

Joining us for the panel:

Seema Amble, Partner at a16z (moderator)
Richard Kopelman, CEO of Aprio
Erik Asgeirsson, President & CEO of CPA.com
Matt Calvano, CEO & Co-Founder of Adaptive
Ali Hussain, CEO & Co-Founder of Tabs
